scope: PR81-104 un-mined HNeRV-family submission slots (PR95 hnerv_muon, PR96 rem2_HNeRV, PR98 hnerv_muon_finetuned_from_pr95, PR100 hnerv_lc_v2, PR101 hnerv_ft_microcodec, PR103 hnerv_lc_ac)
axis_operating_point_note: All EV/byte ranking applied at PR106 r2 frontier (pose_avg ~3.4e-5; pose marginal 2.71x SegNet's per CLAUDE.md 'SegNet vs PoseNet importance - operating-point dependent').

rows.title: hnerv_muon (AaronLeslie138)
rows.author: AaronLeslie138
rows.key_mechanism_description: Muon optimizer (Keller Jordan 2024) for hidden Conv2d weights: Newton-Schulz iteration produces orthogonalized momentum updates via the quintic polynomial X<-aX+(bA+cA**2)X with A=XX^T and (a,b,c)=(3.4445,-4.7750,2.0315). 4D conv weights are flattened to 2D for the NS step; per-step decoupled weight decay applied before the orthogonalized update. Combined with AdamW for stem Linear / RGB heads / biases / latents via partition_params_for_muon. The Muon stage (stage 8 of 8) shapes the post-INT8 weight distribution toward sharper integer grid points - directly improves rate at fixed distortion.
rows.next_action: DEFER - this is a training-time primitive (no archive bytes), but it is the standard-bearer for stage-8 weight-distribution shaping. Surface to grand council alongside training-recipe lane discussion for sub-0.20 contender training.
rows.pr_claimed_score_axis: [contest-CUDA] (per source submission PoseNet/SegNet claims; matches retrospective PR95 root analysis)

rows.title: hnerv_muon (AaronLeslie138)
rows.author: AaronLeslie138
rows.key_mechanism_description: Size-weighted soft categorical entropy regularizer C1a: for each Conv2d/Linear weight tensor, quantize to {-127..127} via Gaussian soft-assignment with bandwidth sigma=0.2 (later 0.1), compute categorical entropy in bits/weight, weight by numel. Stage 5+ adds cat_lambda*c1a_entropy=0.02 (later 0.1) into the training loss. Direct MDL-grounded rate term (Shannon entropy of post-INT8 distribution at integer grid points) - sharpens histogram pre-quantization so the brotli/AC stage compresses better. Differentiable scorer-preprocess-aware per CLAUDE.md eval_roundtrip non-negotiable.
rows.next_action: DEFER - surface to grand council with MacKay seat owing the MDL rate-cost analysis. This is the closest public-PR analogue to a learnable entropy-bottleneck regularizer (Balle 2018 sister); high MDL signal.

rows.title: hnerv_muon (AaronLeslie138)
rows.author: AaronLeslie138
rows.key_mechanism_description: 8-stage loss curriculum for SegNet-aware training: CE -> tau-Softplus(tau=0.3) -> smooth_disagreement (sigmoid-on-negative-margin) -> +QAT (Stage 4) -> +L7_softplus + cat_entropy_v2 (Stage 5; L7-mult=4.0 boost for margin<threshold=1.0 pixels) -> lambda=0.02 (Stage 6) -> sigma=0.1 (Stage 7) -> +Muon (Stage 8). Pose loss is sqrt(10*MSE) constant across all stages. Aggregation: loss = 100*seg + 1*pose + cat_lambda*c1a_entropy. The smooth_disagreement loss bell-curve gradient peaks at margin=0 (pushes boundary pixels across the decision line); the L7 weighting concentrates gradient on hard-to-classify pixels.
rows.next_action: DEFER - surface to grand council as a smooth-disagreement+L7 SegNet curriculum candidate. Sister to existing KL distill but operates in margin-space rather than logit-space.

rows.title: hnerv_muon (AaronLeslie138)
rows.author: AaronLeslie138
rows.key_mechanism_description: Dual-RGB-head HNeRV decoder architecture: 229K params, single-video memorization, 28-d latent-per-pair -> 6 PixelShuffle upsample stages -> dilated-conv refine residual (dilation=2 followed by 3x3 conv) -> SEPARATE rgb_0 and rgb_1 heads producing frame 0 + frame 1 RGB independently from the same upsampled feature map. Channel taper [C,C,C,0.75C,0.58C,0.50C,0.50C]. Per-stage sin() activation. PixelShuffle(2) on Conv(in, out*4, 3x3). The dual-head architecture is the key insight: one decoder forward produces TWO output frames, halving per-frame decoder cost.
rows.next_action: DEFER-pending-research-with-renderer-rescope - if our internal lane plans add a renderer, propose dual-head as a candidate that targets pose+seg simultaneously via shared feature stack. Sister to PR101 hnerv_ft_microcodec which uses the same model bytes.

rows.title: rem2_HNeRV (silver medal cluster - rem2)
rows.author: rem2
rows.key_mechanism_description: Histogram-codec switch byte (single-byte comp_id selects which compressor was used for the AC histograms): 0=lzma, 1=zstd, 2=brotli. Encoder picks the smallest of all 3, writes the chosen ID into the header. This is a cheap multi-codec ensemble at the histogram layer - costs 1 byte per archive, saves whatever the smallest-of-3 wins. Sister to PR101 split-Brotli but more general (handles any compressor family). The decoder dispatches via _decompress_hist(comp_id, blob).
rows.next_action: Port to tac.packet_compiler.pr96_hist_codec_switch (~30 LOC) as a generic 1-byte codec-selector header. Composable with pr103_arithmetic_coding (which currently uses fixed brotli wrapper on histograms).

rows.title: rem2_HNeRV (silver medal cluster - rem2)
rows.author: rem2
rows.key_mechanism_description: uint8-offset-128 RangeDecoder pattern: int8 weights are encoded as their uint8 representation (q + 128 in [0, 256)) so that constriction.stream.model.Categorical can operate on non-negative integers. Decoder does `(ub - 128).astype(np.int8)` after decode. This avoids the signed-vs-unsigned ambiguity that range coders have with negative symbols. Sister to pr103_arithmetic_coding zigzag pattern but simpler (no zigzag, just additive offset).
rows.next_action: Surface to grand council: design tradeoff between uint8-offset-128 (rem2) vs zigzag (PR103). Both are valid sign-encoding strategies for AC; entropy depends on distribution skew. ~20 LOC port if approved.

rows.title: hnerv_muon_finetuned_from_pr95 (EthanYangTW)
rows.author: EthanYangTW
rows.key_mechanism_description: CD1 compact architecture-ordered decoder format: magic=b'CD1' (3 bytes) + scale_bits (1 byte: 16 or 32) + n_tensors (4 bytes), then per-tensor [scale_value (2 or 4 bytes)] + [zigzag-encoded INT8 bytes of size matching state-dict shape]. Tensor NAMES are NOT stored - the decoder reconstructs them from the canonical HNeRVDecoder.state_dict() iteration order. This trades ~30 bytes per tensor (name length + name bytes + 4-byte shape header) for a hardcoded architecture grammar. Specifically targets the schema-known case where the architecture is fixed across submissions.
rows.next_action: Port to tac.packet_compiler.pr98_cd1_compact_decoder (~90 LOC). LENGTH-PREFIXED via state-dict iteration order does NOT violate HNeRV parity lesson 3 (which prohibits fixed BYTE offsets - iteration order is a length-prefixed grammar from the encoder's perspective). Composable with pr100_schema_driven.

rows.title: hnerv_muon_finetuned_from_pr95 (EthanYangTW)
rows.author: EthanYangTW
rows.key_mechanism_description: Decode-side per-frame per-channel constant nudge: at inflate time, AFTER bicubic upsample to camera resolution but BEFORE clamp/round, applies `up[:, 0, 0].sub_(1.0)` (frame-0 red -1), `up[:, 0, 2].sub_(1.0)` (frame-0 blue -1), `up[:, 1, 1].sub_(1.0)` (frame-1 green -1). ZERO archive bytes. Tuned offline against the scorer on the public test corpus. Each nudge is 1 luma unit which is below the scorer's quantization threshold but matters at the rounding boundary. PR102 adds frame-0-red +1. Sister to PR97 H3 grammar concept of decode-side post-processing.
rows.next_action: DEFER-pending-target-mode-declaration - if target_modes=['contest_one_video_replay'] (per CLAUDE.md deterministic packet compiler), nudge is admissible. If target_modes=['contest_generalized'], nudge must be derived from training-set statistics not test-set probing. Surface to grand council.

rows.title: hnerv_lc_v2 (BradyMeighan, gold cluster)
rows.author: BradyMeighan
rows.key_mechanism_description: Schema-driven monolithic-decoder grammar: a SCHEMA list of (tensor_name, shape_tuple) is hardcoded into the decoder source (auto-generated from a reference state_dict). The archive stores ONLY concatenated INT8 codes (brotli'd) + fp16 scales in iteration order. No per-tensor name strings, no per-tensor shape headers. Saves ~30 bytes per tensor (28 tensors * 30 bytes = ~840 bytes) compared to the PR95 self-describing format. The schema is a CODE-side contract; if the architecture changes, the decoder source must be regenerated. Length-prefixed via shape * dtype size at parse time.
rows.next_action: Port to tac.packet_compiler.pr100_schema_driven_decoder_grammar (~70 LOC). The 3 schema-elision variants (PR98 CD1 / PR100 schema.py / PR105 packed-state-schema-size-sorted) are CHOICES not redundancies; surface to grand council for design tradeoff with side-by-side byte-count analysis.

rows.title: hnerv_lc_v2 (BradyMeighan)
rows.author: BradyMeighan
rows.key_mechanism_description: dim=255-sentinel sparse correction sidecar: 2-byte header (u16 n_pairs) + per-pair (u8 dim_idx, i8 delta_q). dim_idx in [0,27] is a valid latent-dim correction; dim_idx=255 means NO correction for that pair (sparse marker). delta_q is i8 scaled by DELTA_SCALE=0.01 (real_delta in [-1.27, 1.27]). Brotli'd. ~1.2 KB per video at 600 pairs even with many sparse-rows (the dim=255 byte stream brotli-compresses well since most pairs are no-ops). Sister to PR101 sidecar but uses byte-sentinel instead of combinatorial-rank for no-op marking.
rows.next_action: Port to tac.packet_compiler.pr100_dim255_sentinel_sidecar (~50 LOC) as a simpler alternative to PR101 combinatorial. Encoder picks the smaller of the two based on no-op fraction. Composable.

rows.title: hnerv_ft_microcodec (GOLD MEDAL, 0.193 contest-CUDA)
rows.author: PR101 author (gold cluster)
rows.key_mechanism_description: DECODER_STORAGE_ORDER = (14, 22, 7, 6, 19, 10, 25, 4, 20, 9, 12, 15, 5, 11, 18, 1, 21, 3, 27, 13, 2, 26, 24, 17, 16, 23, 8, 0): a hand-tuned permutation of the 28 decoder tensors that PUTS HIGHEST-ENTROPY tensors NEXT TO EACH OTHER. The encoder writes tensors in this order; the decoder reads in this order. DECODER_STREAM_ENDS = (1, 2, 22, 23, 26, 27, 28) marks SPLIT-BROTLI boundaries so that low-entropy tensor groups get their own brotli stream (better compression than one big stream). The order is provably-optimal for the PR95 architecture per offline gradient-free search.
rows.next_action: Port the GRAMMAR (not the specific permutation) to tac.packet_compiler.pr101_decoder_storage_order (~50 LOC). The permutation itself must be re-derived per renderer via offline entropy clustering. High EV - this is one of PR101's gold-medal-specific byte savings.

rows.title: hnerv_ft_microcodec (GOLD MEDAL, 0.193 contest-CUDA)
rows.author: PR101 author
rows.key_mechanism_description: CONV4_STORAGE_PERMS: per-tensor 4D-axis permutation table. For each conv tensor index in the storage order, a 4-tuple of axis indices specifying HOW the (out, in, H, W) weight tensor should be reshaped before INT8 zigzag encoding. Examples: tensor 2 -> (3, 0, 2, 1), tensor 14 -> (1, 0, 2, 3), tensor 20 -> (0, 3, 2, 1). The permutation makes spatially-adjacent weights MORE temporally adjacent in the byte stream, improving brotli's LZ77 dictionary hits. CONV4_INVERSE_PERMS is computed at decode time via argsort. Per-tensor optimal permutation derived offline via entropy-vs-permutation search.
rows.next_action: Port the GRAMMAR to tac.packet_compiler.pr101_conv4_storage_perms (~80 LOC). The specific permutations must be re-derived per architecture via 24-perm exhaustive search. High EV - one of PR101's specific byte savings traceable to gold medal.

rows.title: hnerv_ft_microcodec (GOLD MEDAL, 0.193 contest-CUDA)
rows.author: PR101 author
rows.key_mechanism_description: DECODER_BYTE_MAPS: per-tensor sign-encoding strategy selector. Maps INT8 -> UINT8 byte stream using one of {negzig, twos, off}. negzig = zigzag of (-x); twos = two's complement (raw int8 reinterpret); off = signed-byte-offset (x + 128). Each strategy minimizes entropy under a different weight distribution: negzig for highly-skewed-positive, twos for symmetric-around-zero, off for highly-skewed-negative. Per-tensor chosen offline via entropy comparison. Per-tensor table: {9: negzig, 14: negzig, 20: twos, 27: off}. Sister to PR96 uint8-offset-128 but parameterized over 3 strategies.
rows.next_action: Port to tac.packet_compiler.pr101_decoder_byte_maps (~60 LOC). The 3 strategies form a unified taxonomy with pr96; surface to grand council. High EV.

rows.title: hnerv_lc_ac (silver medal cluster - rem2 PR103 follow-up, 0.195)
rows.author: rem2
rows.key_mechanism_description: Merged-AC concatenated RangeDecoder stream: MULTIPLE weight tensors share ONE RangeDecoder. AC_INDICES = [0, 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 21] specifies which 8 SCHEMA-indexed tensors go into the merged stream. The decoder is created once over the full merged_ac bytes; iteratively decode each tensor's symbol_count via its OWN categorical (one histogram per AC tensor). Sharing the arithmetic state across tensors avoids per-tensor RangeDecoder header overhead. Sister to pr103_arithmetic_coding (which uses one decoder per tensor) but more efficient when N tensors share the AC layer.
rows.next_action: Surface to grand council: extend pr103_arithmetic_coding to support multi-tensor merged streams (~90 LOC). High EV at PR106 r2 since rate axis still has byte-saving headroom.

rows.title: hnerv_lc_ac (silver medal cluster, 0.195)
rows.author: rem2
rows.key_mechanism_description: Fixed-section-offset byte parser using LOC-declared length constants: SCA_LEN, BR_LEN=7097, HIST_LEN=895, MERGED_AC_LEN=153856, LATENT_META_LEN, LO_LEN=15537, HI_HIST_LEN=15 are HARDCODED in inflate.py source. parse_archive(blob) walks the byte stream advancing the offset by each fixed constant. NO length prefix at archive parse time (saves ~7 * 4 = 28 bytes of u32 length headers). VIOLATES HNeRV parity lesson 3 fixed-offsets-anti-pattern - any change to encoder bytes requires source rev. This is THE specific design choice that puts PR103 in tension with parity discipline.
rows.next_action: DEFER - port ONLY a length-prefixed variant to tac.packet_compiler.pr103_fixed_offset_or_length_prefixed (~60 LOC). The fixed-offset variant is the exact anti-pattern HNeRV parity lesson 3 prohibits; do NOT port verbatim.
